GateReasons: Boundary ingredients are now well traced with theorem-backed, reduced-theorem-backed, and conditional-theorem-backed statuses; the completion additions remain non-promoted, split across one bounded packaging theorem on explicit split data and one still-assumption-backed Gaussian layer.
The minimal mock-tested boundary interface candidate is I0, but the stronger structural compression still sits inside the current exact WL family rather than a text-level generic theorem.
The split-faithful completion result is improved and strong-safe, but it is still contract-level and does not close the full microscopic completion theorem.

Ledger.Assumptions: Current WL contract ingredients for welded boundary safety and split-faithful completion.
Ledger.Method: Exact source/role classification of the current WL contract ingredients against Main, 2T-LS, and live UOM note labels.
SurrogateBlindSpots: The traceability table is only as fine-grained as the current live theorem/assumption labels.
The completion entries are still non-promoted: one is a bounded packaging theorem on explicit split data, while the Gaussian completion layer remains assumption-backed.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Traceability table for welded boundary and split-faithful completion contracts
Ledger.Acceptance: Every current contract ingredient is mapped to live sources, with boundary ingredients separated from completion additions and classified by interface candidacy.

Ledger.Assumptions: UOM realization, synthetic non-UOM realizations, and a visible-ambiguity negative control.
Ledger.Method: Exact mock-realization audit with one UOM realization, two synthetic non-UOM realizations, and one visible-ambiguity negative control.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Synthetic realizations certify interface shape, not microscopic physics.

Ledger.Source: Main Mathematica interface-search lane plus UOM welded/completion contracts
Ledger.Acceptance: At least one non-UOM realization satisfies the compact-interface search constraints, and a negative control still reproduces visible ambiguity when those constraints fail.

Ledger.Assumptions: Nested boundary-interface candidates I0, I1, I2.
Ledger.Method: Nested candidate search over I0/I1/I2 on exact mock realizations.

Ledger.Source: Main abstract interface lane cross-checked against UOM contract realizations
Ledger.Acceptance: Among the nested candidates, I0 is the smallest theory-neutral boundary interface that still forces the audited boundary canonicalization conclusion on all satisfying mock realizations.

Ledger.Assumptions: Nested completion-aware interface candidates I0, I1, I2.
Ledger.Method: Nested candidate search over I0/I1/I2 for completion-aware safety on exact mock realizations.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Completion-aware minimality remains contract-level and does not close the microscopic SK theorem.

Ledger.Acceptance: Among the nested candidates, I2 is the smallest completion-aware interface that still forces strong-safe completion behavior on all satisfying mock realizations.

Ledger.Assumptions: Five-part strong-safe welded package versus a three-axiom conceptual compression.
Ledger.Method: Exact comparison of the five-part strong-safe package against a three-axiom conceptual compression on the welded ablation family.

Ledger.Source: Main Mathematica welded ablation lane plus UOM compressed welded notes
Ledger.Acceptance: Within the exact audited family, strong-safe is equivalent to exact accepted-record preservation plus a rank-one descended active image plus geometric locking.

Ledger.Assumptions: Current traceability, compact-interface search, and completion-scope caveats.
